codeReview.summary: Core curation flow, error mapping, and persistence are implemented, but the engine does not enforce required cut-segment validity constraints before saving output.

issues.description: LLM-provided segments are accepted without validating that source_file exists in the input transcriptions and that start/end are within the referenced transcription duration (and end > start). _parse_cut_list_response() directly constructs CutSegment objects and only clamps negative durations in total_duration via max(0.0, end-start), so invalid ranges can still be persisted in the cut list.

Multi-pass tests mock Pass 2 with non-JSON text ("Relevant videos identified."), which exercises fallback-to-all behavior instead of the intended relevant-file parsing path. This leaves the Pass 2 selection contract weakly covered.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The backend-worker skill requires manual verification for engine features, but this run records only automated validator commands while still marking followedProcedure=true, suggesting a mismatch between skill procedure and enforcement/reporting.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/backend-worker/SKILL.md:51-55 requires manual verification; handoff 2026-04-10T19-52-29-286Z__curation-engine__9f151315-e328-4269-9607-3f496e419326.json shows only commandsRun at lines 15-35 and skillFeedback.followedProcedure=true at line 153.

summary: Review failed due to a blocking correctness gap: cut segments are not validated against transcription source/bounds before persistence. Non-blocking: multi-pass tests currently miss direct coverage of successful Pass 2 relevant-file parsing.
